hero.title: R.C. Langlands
hero.tagline: Stories shaped by faith and wonder
hero.description: Written for growing readers — earthy characters, fantastical worlds, and secrets waiting to be uncovered.

about: My name is Riley, and I'm a writer and literature teacher from Queensland, Australia. Stories are at the centre of everything I do — I spend my days surrounded by them, sharing them with students in the classroom and quietly building my own on the side.

I grew up in a small Queensland town, and that upbringing never really left me. I still find myself most alive when I'm outside — in the garden, in the bush, somewhere the light is doing something interesting. I cook, I wander, I pay attention to small things. A lot of my best ideas have come from those unhurried moments. I was shaped early by C.S. Lewis and J.R.R. Tolkien — writers who understood that the most extraordinary worlds are the ones that feel, somehow, like they were always there waiting to be found.

I write in images. Atmosphere, mood, the way a place can feel heavy with something unspoken — these are the things that interest me most as a writer. I want my stories to be the kind you carry with you after you've closed the book, where the world and the characters linger a little longer than expected. There's always something underneath the surface in the stories I tell. I hope you'll want to go looking for it.
